story:
I was cleaning my room for the first time in ages—closet, under the bed, windowsill, desk, everywhere—shoving anything useless into a big cardboard box. Oh, my! What a mess. On the back shelf, under tons of old T‑shirts and holey socks, I found my old sneakers. My first impulse was to toss them in, but I paused. They were still in decent shape, a little out of fashion, but sturdy. A bright idea: I’ll take them to my grandparents’ country house! Perfect for muddy paths and garden chores. I set them by my bed, finished the cleaning, collapsed, and fell asleep…

I dreamed I was at my grandparents’ place, searching for those sneakers I had brought not long ago. I couldn’t find them. Instead, I found a small notebook. A diary—written by them. Yes, my old sneakers had left me a message. It read like this…

Day 1
Germany. Hamburg, to be exact. We’d been on display for a month when He came. It felt like being adopted from an orphanage—uncertain but hopeful. Roll the dice! A warm hello to the big world beyond glass. Freedom!!!

One month later
Everything is cool! We walk almost every day. Streets, garages, adventures. So many smells, so many miles. We are happy!

Six months later
A little creased but fine. Winter now. Fewer outings, mostly for workouts. Still, fresh air is fresh air.

One year later
Something’s off. He doesn’t reach for us as much. Déjà vu—like the shop again, only darker, a pantry, a shelf. We know we’re not his only pair. We aren’t jealous. We just miss the road.

One year and three months later
Do you know what it’s like to be forgotten? We do. You wait and hope and tell yourself it’ll be okay, but the door never opens. If we could talk, we’d shout: “We’re ready! Take us out! We’re here!” Maybe we’re not as pretty as before. Maybe we’ve lost some shape. Maybe he prefers Nike or Adidas. It’s fine—just remember us. We were good backup. Remember the abandoned house? The nail that would’ve pierced his foot—our thick sole saved him. And his first date! His legs trembled, but we steadied him with our built‑in arch support. We helped him walk like himself.

The last day
Everything ends sooner or later. Now we live at a country house. Anyone can slip into us to weed the beds. Our closest friends are worms. Fair enough… but we don’t want to stay like this. Let’s go find our way!

I woke up with my heart thumping. First thing, I went to find my old friends—my good, old sneakers. They didn’t look bad at all. Not glamorous, but still them. Why not… I could wear them from time to time, take them back to the country, let them feel the road again. After all, they had carried me through adventures, saved me from sharp nails, and steadied me when it mattered. Some things don’t need to be new to be perfect for the job.
